channel.title: Plex on Kovasky Buezo | Software Developer, Cybersecurity Enthusiast
channel.description: Recent content in Plex on Kovasky Buezo | Software Developer, Cybersecurity Enthusiast

item.title: Intel Arc A380 performance benchmark with no Resizable BAR in a Dell Poweredge T440
item.description: Intro I took a leap of faith by buying an Intel Alchemist series GPU, even after countless people online advised against purchasing one if the target system doesn’t support Resizable BAR. Still, AV1 at this price point felt too compelling to pass up, even if it meant subpar graphical performance. My main intent was transcoding media, so I didn’t think I would mind. I didn’t expect much, but the Intel Arc A380 performed surprisingly well for my needs, showing results close to a GTX 1650.

item.title: Set up a ramdisk for Plex
item.description: Intro Always on the lookout to decrease writes on my consumer SSDs, I stumbled upon the idea of a ramdisk for Plex transcodes. In theory it should offer a performance boost, but in practice I did not really notice any improvements besides lowering SSD writes. Editing fstab Navigate to /etc/fstab and open it for edition. You should see your boot disk and any other mapped media there. Add the following line:
